incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt

steps.text: Check the numbers. What are the two factors being multiplied, and what's their product?

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt

steps.text: Look at the strip. Find 7 times 10. The problem says 7 groups of 10. Place those two factors, then find the product on the strip.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on third or subsequent attempt. System models the answer.

params.description: System places 7 in first slot, 10 in second slot, 70 in product slot.

steps.text: Here's how to build it. 7 times 10 means 7 groups of 10. Those are the factors: 7 and 10. Skip count by 10s seven times: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70. The product is 70. The equation is 7 times 10 equals 70.
